upgrade-audio-to-whisper.objective: Replace the browser-native Web Speech API transcription with OpenAI Whisper (via the /v1/audio/transcriptions endpoint) for all audio recording and transcription features in the application. The new implementation must support streaming blob upload, return word-level timestamps, and degrade gracefully when the Whisper API is unavailable.

platform.stack: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, React 18, Tailwind CSS
platform.architecture-patterns: Server Components for data fetching, Client Components for audio capture, API routes for Whisper proxy

upgrade-audio-to-whisper.current-state: The app uses the browser's built-in SpeechRecognition API (Web Speech API) in src/hooks/useVoiceRecorder.ts. This approach has three known problems: (1) only works in Chrome/Edge; (2) no word-level timestamps; (3) accuracy is poor for non-English input. The current API surface is: startRecording() → stopRecording() → transcript: string. No server-side audio processing exists today.

create.purpose: Whisper API client — use snippet id='whisper-client' verbatim
Authenticated Next.js API route for audio upload — use snippet id='api-route-stub' verbatim

modify.purpose: Replace SpeechRecognition with MediaRecorder + fetch to /api/transcribe; expose words[] alongside transcript string
Wire updated useVoiceRecorder hook; show word-level highlight when words[] available

files.delete: No longer needed — Web Speech API wrapper replaced by Whisper client

upgrade-audio-to-whisper.feature-inventory: - Whisper API client (src/lib/whisper.ts) — verbatim from provided-code snippet - Authenticated /api/transcribe POST route with 401 guard - Updated useVoiceRecorder hook: MediaRecorder → blob → Whisper → { transcript, words } - Word-level timestamp passthrough to VoiceButton component - Graceful degradation: if Whisper API call fails, return empty words and surface error message - Removal of legacy Web Speech API wrapper

principles.principle: The two provided-code snippets (whisper-client, api-route-stub) are used verbatim. Do not rewrite, rename, or restructure them. They represent the exact interface the rest of the codebase will consume.
Every server-side audio path must authenticate before processing. The api-route-stub already implements this via auth(); never bypass it.
If the Whisper call throws, catch the error, log it server-side, and return a 503 with a user-friendly message. The client hook must handle 503 without crashing the UI.

flow-design.steps: 1. Create src/lib/whisper.ts from snippet whisper-client (verbatim). 2. Create src/app/api/transcribe/route.ts from snippet api-route-stub (verbatim). 3. Refactor useVoiceRecorder.ts: swap SpeechRecognition for MediaRecorder, POST blob to /api/transcribe, expose { transcript, words } state. 4. Update VoiceButton.tsx to consume the new hook shape and render word highlights. 5. Delete src/lib/speechRecognition.ts. 6. Run full test suite; fix TypeScript errors.

technical-requirements.state-management: useVoiceRecorder must expose: isRecording: boolean, transcript: string, words: { word: string; start: number; end: number }[], error: string | null. State resets on startRecording().
technical-requirements.data-flow: User clicks record → MediaRecorder collects chunks → stopRecording() blobs chunks → POST FormData to /api/transcribe → Whisper response → hook state updated → VoiceButton re-renders with transcript + word highlights.
technical-requirements.edge-cases: - Empty recording (user stops immediately): return empty transcript, empty words[]. - Whisper API rate-limit (429): surface "Too many requests — try again shortly." - Network offline: surface "No network connection." - Audio codec unsupported by Whisper: convert to audio/wav via AudioContext before upload.
technical-requirements.security: - OPENAI_API_KEY must never be exposed to the client. It lives only in .env.local and is accessed exclusively in src/lib/whisper.ts (server-only). - The /api/transcribe route enforces auth() — unauthenticated requests get 401. - FormData size limit: enforce 10 MB max in the route handler.

do-not.rule: Do NOT call the Whisper API from client-side code — always proxy through /api/transcribe.
Do NOT modify the existing auth() implementation in src/lib/auth.ts.
Do NOT change the external interface of VoiceButton.tsx props — only internal wiring changes.
Do NOT add new npm dependencies beyond the already-installed openai package.
Do NOT keep any import of SpeechRecognition or webkitSpeechRecognition after migration.
Do NOT expose OPENAI_API_KEY in any client bundle or API response.

acceptance-criteria.check: npm run build completes with zero TypeScript errors.
POST /api/transcribe with a valid audio blob and valid session returns 200 with { text, words }.
useVoiceRecorder.words is non-empty after a successful recording of spoken English.
No reference to SpeechRecognition or webkitSpeechRecognition remains in the codebase (grep check).
When Whisper API returns 503, the UI shows an error message without crashing.

implementation-instructions.step: Create src/lib/whisper.ts using snippet id="whisper-client" verbatim. Verify: file exists, content matches snippet exactly.
Create src/app/api/transcribe/route.ts using snippet id="api-route-stub" verbatim. Verify: file exists, imports from @/lib/whisper.
Refactor src/hooks/useVoiceRecorder.ts. Remove all SpeechRecognition usage. Add MediaRecorder-based recording. Add fetch call to /api/transcribe. Expose { transcript, words, isRecording, error }.
Update src/components/VoiceButton.tsx to consume the new hook shape. Render word-level highlights when words[] is non-empty.
Run: npm run build. Fix any TypeScript errors. Run: npm test. Fix failures. Verify acceptance criteria 1–7.
